questions.question: Explain the difference between user mode and kernel mode in operating system operations.
expectedPoints: User mode has restricted privileges and cannot execute privileged instructions
Kernel mode has full privileges and can execute any instruction
Mode switching occurs through system calls or interrupts
Dual-mode operation protects the system from user program errors

questions.question: Describe the complete process of handling a system call, from the user program request to returning control back to the program.
expectedPoints: User program prepares parameters and executes trap instruction
CPU switches from user mode to kernel mode
OS identifies the requested service using system call number
OS executes the requested operation in kernel mode
OS prepares return values and switches back to user mode
Control returns to user program with results
